{"key_event":"Plan Colombia and Democratic Security","reasoning":"By 2008 the state had substantially recovered coercive and territorial capacity through Plan Colombia, professionalized forces, improved intelligence, and major setbacks to FARC, though paramilitary capture and successor violence kept it below a fully strong state. Civil society gained from safer roads, courts, media, NGOs, victims’ activism, and anti-FARC marches, but mass displacement, land grabs, intimidation, and parapolitics limited the net improvement.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":5.5,"state_change":1.1,"state_power":6.8}
